issue_title: Look around window cannot be set to 0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Since keycloak 24 the `Look around window` configuration option in the OTP policies (Authentication ⟶ Policies ⟶ OTP policy) cannot be configured to 0. That property is the extra time periods (TOTP) or counts (HOTP) the server will take into account just in case the generator and server are out of sync. So the value 0 makes sense (no extra time windows for TOTP and no extra counts for HOTP).

The configuration for OTP is from 0 to any max number with default 1 ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/25.0.2/js/apps/admin-ui/src/authentication/policies/OtpPolicy.tsx#L178)). So 0 should be allowed.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Value 0 should be allowed.

### Actual behavior

When you set 0 it converts to default 1.

### How to Reproduce?

Just go to Authentication ⟶ Policies ⟶ OTP policy in your realm. The value for the look around window is 1 by default. Click the minus buttom, it's still 1 and not 0.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: SAML adapter IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener not executed when session ID changes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

adapter/jee-saml

### Describe the bug

In the SAML elytron adapter the `IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener` is not executed by default. There are two differences with the old undertow adapter:

1. In undertow adapter the listener is added always (see [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/24.0.6/adapters/saml/undertow/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/undertow/ServletSamlAuthMech.java#L59)) but in the elytron adapter it is not added by default ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/25.0.2/adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/KeycloakConfigurationServletListener.java#L165)). In the elytron adapter the updater is only added if `keycloak.sessionIdMapperUpdater.classes` init parameter is added to the context. This is not expected and the documentation just comments about this parameter to change the updater ([doc](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.2/securing_apps/#_saml_logout_in_cluster)).
2. The second difference is the undertow adapter updates the mapping when session ID changes ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/24.0.6/adapters/saml/undertow/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/undertow/IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener.java#L75)) but the elytron counter part does not implement `HttpSessionIdListener` and therefore if the session ID is modified the mapping is lost and the user needs to login (no user interaction) again.

Both are bugs that should be fixed.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When the session ID changes the class `IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener` updates the session mapping OK and the session is not lost and the user is not enforced to do the login dance again.

### Actual behavior

The session is lost in the mapper and the user should go to the login page to silently login again.

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy the SAML adapter via galleon layers in wildfly. Create a sample application that calls the `request. changeSessionId()` method to change the session ID in the app. The next user interaction performs the login again, creating the saml request, esponse and so on and so forth. As commented the session in keycloak is OK so the user needs no interaction (cookie) for the login.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
SAML IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener should be added always and must implement HttpSessionIdListener interface
Closes #32084

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Remember Me with External Infinispan is not works properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

We are using v23.0.7 with external infinispan v14.0.21

**SSO Session Idle Remember Me**  value does not respect set value from UI.

When I check the core I found [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e13c9bf46297303b1457ef07bbcab378e502f3c7/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/remotestore/RemoteCacheInvoker.java#L86) line of code.

Because of that  it sets session cache **maxidletimeseconds** value to **SSO Session Max + 30 min**

**UI Values :**

```
SSO Session Idle : 5 min
SSO Session Max : -29 min (minus)
SSO Session Idle Remember Me : 30 min
SSO Session Max Remember Me : 60 min
```

and the related session cache entry is :

```
{
  "cache-control" : [ "max-age=59" ],
  "cluster-backup-owners" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-1-26300" ],
  "cluster-node-name" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-0-47692" ],
  "cluster-primary-owner" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-2-32175" ],
  "cluster-server-address" : [ "[10.233.88.151:7800]" ],
  "content-length" : [ "0" ],
  "content-type" : [ "application/x-jboss-marshalling" ],
  "created" : [ "1723563920995" ],
  "etag" : [ "1512539968" ],
  "expires" : [ "Tue, 13 Aug 2024 15:47:35 GMT" ],
  "last-modified" : [ "Tue, 13 Aug 2024 15:45:20 GMT" ],
  "lastused" : [ "1723563995737" ],
  "maxidletimeseconds" : [ "60" ],
  "timetoliveseconds" : [ "35999" ]
}
```

Because of hard coded value of :

This line `context.maxIdleTimeLoader.getMaxIdleTimeMs(realm)` somehow returns the **SSO Session Max** not idle time.

`final long maxIdleTimeMs = loadedMaxIdleTimeMs + 1800000;`

maxidletimeseconds is 60 sec. And that is understandable.

### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

maxidletimeseconds should get its value from **SSO Session Idle Remember Me** when I set it

### Actual behavior

It gets from hard coded value

### How to Reproduce?

Explained

### Anything else?

_No response_

```
2024-08-13 10:52:29,686 INFO  [tc.qua.io/.0] (docker-java-stream-693675543) STDOUT: 	at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper._readMapAndClose(ObjectMapper.java:4899)
2024-08-13 10:52:29,687 INFO  [tc.qua.io/.0] (docker-java-stream-693675543) STDOUT: 	at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper.readValue(ObjectMapper.java:3846)
2024-08-13 10:52:29,687 INFO  [tc.qua.io/.0] (docker-java-stream-693675543) STDOUT: 	at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper.readValue(ObjectMapper.java:3814)

2024-08-13 10:52:29,687 INFO  [tc.qua.io/.0] (docker-java-stream-693675543) STDOUT: 	... 12 more
```

## Cause

The field `fetchRoles` is currently always sent by the admin client due the fact that it is marked on the class `RolePolicyRepresentation` as primitive `boolean` . Updating to `Boolean` similarly like all other representation objects are using (See for example `RealmRepresentation`) makes it possible that admin-client does not include the field (if not set explicitly) and hence makes it possible to create the role in Keycloak 24 where the field `fetchRoles` does not yet exists on the `RolePolicyRepresentation`.


### Version

admin-client nightly with Keycloak server 24.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It is possible to create rolePolicy with newest admin-client to Keycloak 24.0.5 (if the field `fetchRoles` is omitted)


### Actual behavior

It is possible to create rolePolicy with newest admin-client to Keycloak 24.0.5 (if the field `fetchRoles` is omitted)

### How to Reproduce?

See the description

### Anything else?

Related PR to keycloak-client: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-client/pull/22
commit_message:
Update RolePolicyRepresentation fields from 'boolean' to 'Boolean'
closes #32117

issue_title: Offline session bug on 25.0.2
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

There's a problem in "version" column of above entities, that results in error when user tries to login in a offline session.

**- PersistentUserSessionEntity.java**
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/PersistentUserSessionEntity.java

**- PersistentClientSessionEntity.java**
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/PersistentClientSessionEntity.java


Keycloak tries to query version in lowercase, but in database the version column is UPPERCASE

```
select top ? puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG,puse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID,puse1_0.BROKER_SESSION_ID,puse1_0.CREATED_ON,puse1_0.DATA,puse1_0.LAST_SESSION_REFRESH,puse1_0.REALM_ID,puse1_0.USER_ID,puse1_0.version from OFFLINE_USER_SESSION puse1_0 where puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG=? and puse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID=? and puse1_0.REALM_ID=?

select pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER,pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG,pcse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID,pcse1_0.DATA,pcse1_0.TIMESTAMP,pcse1_0.version from OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION pcse1_0 where ((pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID=? and pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER=? and pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID=? and pcse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG=? and pcse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID=?))

```

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Entities with version field with correct casing for successful login on offline sessions

### Actual behavior

Throws "For mode on this error consult the server log at the debug level"

But i forget to save the errors

### How to Reproduce?

In my case i was on version 24.0.1 and updated to 24.0.2, using Microsoft SQL Server

### Anything else?

_No response_

Specify version column name in a case-sensitive manner (#32169)

Closes #32127

issue_title: UserId too long to add Security Key WebauthN

### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

authentication/webauthn

Same issue as https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/24645. I could not reopen the other 

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Ability to add security keys for federated users. Note that it may be needed to simulate this with LDAP provider with import disabled (To make sure that userId is in longer format like `f:<ldap provider UUID>:<ldap UUID>` or might be simulated with custom userStorage provider.

Error: Security key registration result is invalid. TypeError: User handle exceeds 64 bytes.

 Add `User federation` ldap should work well if the `username` is set to `userPrincipalName`.

I don't think that WebAuthn will change their standards which leaves it to Keycloak to adjust. Here is the link to the specification https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#sctn-createCredential. It fails on point 5.

issue_title: Remote Infinispan code must not call JPA code in non-blocking thread
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When showing the list of sessions, the data for the users maybe loaded from the database inside the HotRod client async pool. 

This is problematic in at least two ways: 
* JPA access is blocking, and shouldn't be called from the event loop
* The JTA transaction manager doesn't know about the transaction, as that is only bound to the main thread

main

Users should be loaded from the database when showing the session list

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak with remote-cache enabled, so user sessions are stored and retrieved from the remote Infinispan
* Create an admin and a regular user
* Create log in with both users concurrently (possibly and incognito window for the second user)
* Restart Keycloak to clear the caches, so the session list will need to load the user from the database
* Login as admin
* Show the realm's session list -> exception will be thrown on the session that is not the current session / user. 

(it is a bit simpler to reproduce if you inject a code change that a user is no longer found with `updater.getValue().getUserId() + "x"` ... but beware, if the user is not found it, the user session will be removed from the remote store) 

### Anything else?

I'll create a draft PR. I don't have an overview on the reactive code used in the remote store, so I'll need additional help to ensure that there aren't any other places.

issue_title: SingleSelect-kind readonly attribute is not disabled in account console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When having a single select kind of user attribute as readonly, the input is not being disabled and the user can still choose a different value from the list (incorrect) - however they can't submit it (correct). I believe this is due to the `isDisabled`-Parameter present in `KeycloakSelect` not being utilised to set the `disabled`-attribute in `SingleSelect` (and I believe it's the same in `TypeaheadSelect`).

I hope I described this correctly, front end isn't my forte.

### Version

25

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user cannot alter the value / choose a value in a readonly single select attribute. 

### Actual behavior

The single select readonly attribute is not disabled and the user can therefore choose a different value in the form.

### How to Reproduce?

Set a single select attribute (with "inputType: select"-annotation), create user, make it readonly, login as the user.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Revisit Infinispan session idle and lifetime settings
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

(Ticket might later be moved to the Keycloak main repository)

We put user session into the cache with a max lifetime and an idle time. If we retrieve them, IMHO the idle time will be reset, and they will be removed once they are either idle or expired. 

At the same time we're updating the entries whenever they are refreshed. So I wonder that the idle time should always be the same as the expiration time from the perspective of Infinispan. Keycloak will then on the next refresh update the lifetime and the expiry time with a new value.

This would also help us to prevent reading entries from the cache which then turn out to be expired from Keycloak's perspective.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Session entries in Infinispan should be removed when they are not refreshed - independent of when and how many times they are read from the cache.

### Actual behavior

There is an idle time set to the refresh interval, and a max lifetime setting set to the maximum lifetime of the session.

### How to Reproduce?

Look at the UI entries in the external Infinispan.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Bootstrap options missing from help
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The bootstrap options are not shown in help. This applies to CLI help (`kc.sh start|start-dev --help`) as well as in the `All configuration guide`.

This includes at least the following options:

* `bootstrap-admin-username`
* `bootstrap-admin-password`
* `bootstrap-admin-client-id`
* `bootstrap-admin-client-secret`

This makes it hard to use these options as the only place to discover them in the `Admin bootstrap and recovery` guide. As I can't seem to get these options quite right, or forget them, I need to then open the guide instead of just looking at the help for reference.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`All configuration` guide has a `Bootstrap` section, which lists the options. 

`kc.sh start|start-dev --help` lists a `Bootstrap` section, which lists the options.

### Actual behavior

The bootstrap options are hidden from both the `All configuration` guide and CLI help

### How to Reproduce?

* Run `./kc.sh start-dev --help | grep bootstrap` 
* Observe no options are listed

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: `show-config` command outputs duplicate options
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When a CLI format of a config option name contains a hyphen (`-`) and such option is specified as an env var, the `show-config` command outputs this option twice – once in the correct format with the hyphen, and second where hyphens are replaced by dots. This is particularly problematic when additional processing of the options needs to be done, like masking security sensitive values which work only for options with the correct name (with hyphens).

### Version

main,25

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

For example:
```
Current Configuration:
	kc.hostname-admin =  foobar (ENV)
	...
```

### Actual behavior

For example:
```
Current Configuration:
	kc.hostname-admin =  foobar (ENV)
	kc.hostname.admin =  foobar (ENV)
	...
```

### How to Reproduce?

```
KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN=foobar ./kc.sh show-config
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Migration to persistent sessions fails from Keycloak version <22
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

This PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/17525 introduced a new client session note `clientId`. This means all client sessions created in KC 22 or newer have this note. However, the sessions created by an older version do not have this note.

The persistent user sessions migration is relying on that note to be present and if it is not there, the migration fails with:
```
..."java.lang.NullPointerException","message":"Cannot invoke \"String.startsWith(String)\" because \"id\" is null"...
```
as reported here: 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/28271#discussioncomment-10351978


### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The migration works even if client sessions were created by an older KC version

### Actual behavior

The migration fails

### How to Reproduce?

1. Run KC 21 and create some sessions
2. Run KC 25 with enabled `persistent-user-sessions` feature
3. Migration fails

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

On discussion #31265 I reported this bug.

The scenario:
- I login using the authorization_code flow via keycloak login page 
- My application issues a new token
- Via keycloak administrative console, I force that user session logout
- At this moment the backchannel logout triggers, and my app stores the SID claim of the logout_token  in a blacklist to guarantee that all tokens issued with that SID will be denied.
- When I navigate again to the keycloak login page, the browser session uses the same SID via AUTH_SESSION_ID from the previous login (the cookie was restored/reused).
- After enter with my credentials, another token will be issued (by my app) with that same SID, and my app will deny this new token because that SID was already logged out

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

In my opinion, Keycloak should not accepting SIDs (via AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie) that were already logged out.

Sessions "reborn" that was already dead (logged out) is a strange behavior, in my opinion.

This turns in a complex token management, my application will need to also to keep the "iat" claim of the logout_token, to be possible to accept or deny tokens with a specific SID.

For example, with the current behavior, my application will need to validate access tokens in this way:
- the SID of the token were logged out in some moment?
- the "iat" claim of the access token is after the SID logout moment? If so, I will accept it. Otherwise, I will deny it.

### Actual behavior

As mentioned in the scenario in description.

### How to Reproduce?

As mentioned in the scenario in description.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: error message "Address already in use" should state which address/port in particular
issue_body:
### Description

On  keycloack 25.0.2
```
KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin bin/kc.sh start-dev
2024-08-16 13:42:03,529 INFO  [org.infinispan.CONTAINER] (ForkJoinPool.commonPool-worker-1) ISPN000556: Starting user marshaller 'org.infinispan.jboss.marshalling.core.JBossUserMarshaller'
2024-08-16 13:42:04,285 INFO  [org.keycloak.broker.provider.AbstractIdentityProviderMapper] (main) Registering class org.keycloak.broker.provider.mappersync.ConfigSyncEventListener
2024-08-16 13:42:04,366 INFO  [org.keycloak.connections.infinispan.DefaultInfinispanConnectionProviderFactory] (main) Node name: node_625156, Site name: null
2024-08-16 13:42:05,298 INFO  [com.arjuna.ats.jbossatx] (main) ARJUNA032014: Stopping transaction recovery manager
2024-08-16 13:42:05,347 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Failed to start server in (development) mode
2024-08-16 13:42:05,347 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Unable to start HTTP server
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Unable to start the management interface
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Unable to start the management interface
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Address already in use
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) For more details run the same command passing the '--verbose' option. Also you can use '--help' to see the details about the usage of the particular command.
```

the error `ERROR: Address already in use` I guess it's correct but it does not help me to find what in particular is failing. 

I eventually figured out that keycloak by default is using :8080 and :9000 so it had to be one of those.  So running `bin/kc.sh start-dev --http-management-port 9002` got rid of the problem. 

But I think the error message should be improve like  this 

```
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Address already in use (0.0.0.0:9000) 

```

or 
```
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348  INFO [io.quarkus] (main) Trying management interface on http://0.0.0.0:9000.
2024-08-16 13:42:05,348 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Address already in use
```



### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Well, it will save some time and confusion specially for beginners, that may not understand that keycloack is using other ports beside 8080.

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: SAML Frontchannel Logout missing via Redirect or Post Binding is missing signature if login happened via artifact binding
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

Given you are using a SAML Client using the artifact binding for login and a configured frontchannel-logout.
The front-channel logout URLS of the SAML client have a configuration value in either POST or Redirect logout URL.

The request generation in the SAML-Protocol, takes the Session Note of the login Binding into account to consider, whether the logout request needs to be signed, even though the binding of the logout is configured differently.

In that case the logout request fails due to a missing signature.

### Version

25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Once the logout process determined which Binding to use for logout, the logout dedicated information should be used.
Therefore the logout process will be signed, in case it is not an artifact binding, and document signing is configured for the client.

### Actual behavior

The signing of the logout request is skipped, when the login request happened using the artifact binding.

### How to Reproduce?

You will need a SAML service provider capable of doing logins using the artifact binding and front-channel logouts using the POST or Redirect binding. The SAML- SP must require logout requests to be signed and the Login of the SP needs to use the artifact binding

Given that software you configure a client with the following configuration
- The client must not have POST or Artifact binding enforced.
- Sign documents needs to be activated 
- Front Channel Logout is activated 
- A "Logout Service Redirect Binding URL" or "Logout Service POST Binding URL" is configured
- Artifact Binding URL and Artifact Resolution Service URLs are configured.

Steps to produce the issue:

1. Login (successfully) to the SP via the IP, so there is a session not as Artifact Binding
2. Logout with that user, so a front channel logout is send to the SP.
3. The logout will fail, as the SP complains about a missing request signature.

### Anything else?

The line in question to be found here: 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/95e649f457a83c3ce61d8aad0626754d8027a8bd/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/saml/SamlProtocol.java#L709
commit_message:
fix missing signature for SAML frontChannel Logout

In case of frontchannel logout combined with artifact-Binding on login Keycloak did not sign the requests, even though "sign documents" was configured.

Closes #32262

Signed-off-by: Steffen Ritter <steffen.ritter@bare.id>

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

`org.keycloak.events` is a special category that should only be used for events, and not general log statements.

In addition using `JBossLoggingEventListenerProvider` for this purpose is not good. It's an optional thing that is only enabled for some realms, and this just isn't the correct place for this statement.

`org.keycloak.events` only logs login events or admin events, and not general log statements.

Invalid event logged using `org.keycloak.events`:

```
2024-08-21 11:11:43,933 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-1) admin is a temporary admin service account. To harden security, create a permanent account and delete the temporary one.
```

### How to Reproduce?

Login with a temporary user account

_No response_

issue_title: KeycloakRealmImport not working with Istio service mesh
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

I am installing Keycloak with the Operator in a namespace that has Istio sidecar injection enabled.
When I create a KeycloakRealmImport, the pod gets stuck in a NotReady state.

### Version

25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The realm should be imported into the Keycloak server.

### Actual behavior

The pod is stuck in a NotReady state.

```
$ kubectl get pods -A
NAMESPACE      NAME                                    READY   STATUS     RESTARTS        AGE
istio-system   istio-ingressgateway-75fcb94949-9pmw5   1/1     Running    0               3m48s
istio-system   istiod-5f7944b777-t7wc4                 1/1     Running    0               3m55s
keycloak       example-count0-kc-lvbqt                 1/2     NotReady   0               2m7s
keycloak       example-kc-0                            2/2     Running    0               2m47s
keycloak       keycloak-operator-54bcb5c9f7-bdhjs      2/2     Running    0               3m6s
kube-system    coredns-7db6d8ff4d-f9j5t                1/1     Running    0               3m55s
kube-system    etcd-minikube                           1/1     Running    0               4m9s
kube-system    kube-apiserver-minikube                 1/1     Running    0               4m9s
kube-system    kube-controller-manager-minikube        1/1     Running    0               4m10s
kube-system    kube-proxy-vfcgm                        1/1     Running    0               3m55s
kube-system    kube-scheduler-minikube                 1/1     Running    0               4m9s
kube-system    storage-provisioner                     1/1     Running    1 (3m24s ago)   4m8s
postgresql     postgresql-db-0                         2/2     Running    0               3m6s
```

### How to Reproduce?

```
#!/bin/bash


# Delete old minikube clusters
minikube delete --all

# Start minikube
minikube start --cpus=2 --memory 2g

# Download and install Istio
curl -L https://istio.io/downloadIstio | sh -
./istio-1.23.0/bin/istioctl install -y

# Create Keycloak namespace and enable Istio injection
kubectl create namespace keycloak
kubectl label namespace keycloak istio-injection=enabled

# Install Keycloak CRDs
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keycloak/keycloak-k8s-resources/25.0.4/kubernetes/keycloaks.k8s.keycloak.org-v1.yml -n keycloak
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keycloak/keycloak-k8s-resources/25.0.4/kubernetes/keycloakrealmimports.k8s.keycloak.org-v1.yml -n keycloak
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keycloak/keycloak-k8s-resources/25.0.4/kubernetes/kubernetes.yml -n keycloak

# Install PostgreSQL
kubectl create namespace postgresql
kubectl label namespace postgresql istio-injection=enabled
kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgresql-db
  namespace: postgresql
spec:
  serviceName: postgresql-db-service
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: postgresql-db
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: postgresql-db
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgresql-db
          image: postgres:16
          volumeMounts:
            - mountPath: /data
              name: cache-volume
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: keycloak
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              value: keycloak
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /data/pgdata
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: keycloak
      volumes:
        - name: cache-volume
          emptyDir: {}
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres-db
  namespace: postgresql
spec:
  selector:
    app: postgresql-db
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
  - port: 5432
    targetPort: 5432
EOF

# Create PostgreSQL secret for Keycloak
kubectl create secret generic keycloak-db-secret -n keycloak \
  --from-literal=username=keycloak \
  --from-literal=password=keycloak

# Create Keycloak instance
kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
  name: example-kc
  namespace: keycloak
  labels:
    app: keycloak
spec:
  instances: 1
  db:
    vendor: postgres
    host: postgres-db.postgresql.svc
    usernameSecret:
      name: keycloak-db-secret
      key: username
    passwordSecret:
      name: keycloak-db-secret
      key: password
  http:
    httpEnabled: true
  hostname:
    strict: false
  proxy:
    headers: xforwarded
  ingress:
      enabled: false
EOF

# Deploy sample realm
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keycloak/keycloak/main/operator/src/test/resources/example-realm.yaml -n keycloak

```


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Multiple Logout Confirmation Actions Trigger NullPointerExceptions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We are finding since upgrading from Keycloak 24 to 25.0.2 and now 25.0.4, that we are seeing multiple calls to the `POST logout-confirm` endpoint.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a65508ca13da5ad41ae0f4aa045ae0dd6219f3dc/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/LogoutEndpoint.java#L355

One of the calls succeeds, but the other calls fail with a NPE:
```
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.hashCode()" because "id" is null
	at org.keycloak.services.DefaultKeycloakSession.getProvider(DefaultKeycloakSession.java:187)
	at org.keycloak.models.KeycloakBeanProducer_ProducerMethod_getKeycloakSession_XXX_ClientProxy.getProvider(Unknown Source)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationProcessor.getClientData(AuthenticationProcessor.java:296)
	at org.keycloak.services.util.AuthenticationFlowURLHelper.getLastExecutionUrl(AuthenticationFlowURLHelper.java:93)
	at org.keycloak.services.util.AuthenticationFlowURLHelper.showPageExpired(AuthenticationFlowURLHelper.java:55)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.SessionCodeChecks.showPageExpired(SessionCodeChecks.java:478)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.SessionCodeChecks.initialVerify(SessionCodeChecks.java:317)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logoutConfirmAction(LogoutEndpoint.java:365)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$logoutConfirmAction_xxx.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

### Version

25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

There should be mechanisms in the UI page to limit users accidentally clicking the logout confirmation multiple times.

Server side, there should be some locking or other mechanism to limit concurrent calling of this endpoint for the same client_id & tab_id. Maybe return with some 4XX status code response in this case, instead of attempting to proceed and failing in unexpected ways resulting with a 500 response.

### Actual behavior

It is undetermined what a user experiences when this happens. The browser may show success, or there might be an error page.

### How to Reproduce?

The problem happens when POST-ing to the `logout-confirm` endpoint simultaneously providing the same client_id and tab_id as query parameters.

### Anything else?

_No response_

closes #32435

issue_title: "Cookie not found" in multi-step auth flows / mobile browsers
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We have a multi-step auth flow which results in Keycloak using internal URLs for each step / execution. If a user without KC_RESTART and AUTH_SESSION_ID cookies opens this URL directly (for example because he closed his browser and re-opens it later with the browser remembering the last URL - a common issue on mobile devices), Keycloak displays a "Cookie not found" error.
This error basically means that the user has to start from scratch but we see from feedback of our users, that they dont understand that / think that our site is broken.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user should be redirected to the client where he can start the flow from scratch (or do other things). This would be the same behavior as "already_logged_in" or "temporarily_unavailable".

### Actual behavior

Keycloak returns a "Cookie not found" error page.

### How to Reproduce?

Make sure to get to a subpage of the login flow by either configuring a multi-step flow or entering a wrong user/password once in the default flow. Then delete the KC_RESTART and AUTH_SESSION_ID cookies and reload the page.

Alternatively do it like our users in a browser which remembers the last opened URL (like most mobile browsers do) and close+reopen the browser after you are on the flow-sub-page.

### Anything else?

I propose to handle this error like "already_logged_in" here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/4d621e5dfd814586aaa2166886f6f15052fbd972/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/SessionCodeChecks.java#L199-L207

-> Use the client_data, validate it against the client-configuration in Keycloak and redirect to to given redirect-uri with error=temporarily_unavailable
commit_message:
Cookie not found in multi-step auth flows / mobile browsers
Closes #32462

Signed-off-by: Douglas Palmer <dpalmer@redhat.com>

### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Identity provider secret is visible in Identity Providers tab on organization page (in API request).
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d5146ab2-7013-469c-a2b1-4b334e8e4e7f)

We can see Identity Provider secret in **/admin/realms/{realm}/organizations/{organizationId}/identity-providers** request that loads organization's IdProviders.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76570b74-41b9-464b-b5a5-472ac964e8d1)

Identity provider password is normally not visible in Identity Providers tab. 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ad66d2e-c314-4f8d-b4b3-6afb41cdfc31)


### Version

nightly 

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Identity provider secret/password shouldn't be shown. 

### Actual behavior

Identity provider secret/password is visible.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create realm
2. Enable organizations
3. Add identity provider, e.g. Microsoft and fill it with some data (even random)
4. Create organization
5. Assign Identity Provider to the organization
6. Go outside organization page.
7. Open dev tools
8. Go back to organization page. Go to identity providers tab.
9. You can see provider secret in **identity-providers** request

### Anything else?

There are also some boolean properties in IdentityProvider.Config models that are stored as strings. I suppose it's not correct;

kc.org.broker.public: "true"
kc.org.broker.redirect.mode.email-matches: "true"
commit_message:
Identity Provider secret visible in Organization tab (API request)

Closes #32486

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>

issue_title: Failing operator test - KeycloakDeploymentTest.testPodNamePropagation
issue_body:
### Description

In several job runs, inluding https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/10594540860/job/29358667097, KeycloakDeploymentTest.testPodNamePropagation is failing due to an underlying exception of

Caused by: jakarta.enterprise.inject.UnsatisfiedResolutionException: No bean found for required type [class io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.tracing.DelayedAttributes] and qualifiers [[@jakarta.enterprise.inject.Any()]]

This is not consistently occurring nor has it been reproducable locally. Locally I consistenly observe TracerProducer providing the DelayedAttributes regardless of where other breakpoints are set in attempts to alter the startup.

cc @geoand @mabartos
commit_message:
OTEL: Fix #podNamePropagation test for Operator (#35769)

Fixes #32490

Signed-off-by: Martin Bartoš <mabartos@redhat.com>
